meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Azure MLOps Engineer Associate (AI-300)
meta.title: MS-AI300-3: Pipeline Reuse, Deployment Strategies, and Monitoring

questions.stem: Which Azure Machine Learning compute type is best suited to an individual engineer interactively developing notebooks?

options.text: A compute instance
A managed online endpoint
A batch endpoint
A model registry

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Compute instances are intended for interactive development such as notebooks and ad hoc authoring.

**B** is incorrect because Online endpoints serve inference traffic, not notebook authoring.

**C** is incorrect because Batch endpoints run asynchronous scoring jobs.

**D** is incorrect because A registry stores shareable assets rather than interactive compute.

[Azure ML compute targets](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-compute-target)

questions.stem: A training workload must scale out for repeated jobs and stop when idle to control cost. Which compute target is the best default choice?

options.text: A compute cluster
A compute instance
A local laptop attached as a target
A model registry

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Compute clusters are the standard elastic target for scheduled and repeated training jobs.

**B** is incorrect because Instances are better for interactive work than pooled training jobs.

**C** is incorrect because A local machine is not the best managed default for scalable production jobs.

**D** is incorrect because Registries are not execution targets.

[Azure ML compute targets](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-compute-target)

questions.stem: Why are Azure ML pipeline components valuable in MLOps?

options.text: They make steps reusable and versionable across pipelines
They remove the need for environments
They are only for low-code Designer use
They automatically deploy every model to production

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Components package inputs, outputs, commands, and environments so steps can be reused and versioned.

**B** is incorrect because Components still reference environments.

**C** is incorrect because They are usable from YAML and SDK, not only Designer.

**D** is incorrect because Deployment remains a separate stage.

[Azure ML pipelines and components](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-create-component-pipelines-cli)

questions.stem: A team wants the same pipeline definition to run against different input paths and hyperparameters without editing code. Which design supports that best?

options.text: Declare pipeline inputs and runtime parameters
Hard-code every path and setting in the script
Create a new workspace for each dataset
Use only manual Studio clicks

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Pipeline parameters let the same definition run flexibly across environments and inputs.

**B** is incorrect because Hard-coding hurts reuse.

**C** is incorrect because Separate workspaces are unnecessary for parameter variation.

**D** is incorrect because Manual clicks are not the most reproducible design.

[Azure ML pipelines and components](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-create-component-pipelines-cli)

questions.stem: Which data asset type is intended for tabular or structured data with schema semantics in Azure ML?

options.text: MLTable
URI file
Docker environment
Managed online endpoint

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because MLTable is designed for structured datasets with transformations and schema information.

**B** is incorrect because URI file is a generic file reference rather than a tabular abstraction.

**C** is incorrect because An environment packages software dependencies.

**D** is incorrect because An online endpoint is for inference serving.

[Azure ML data concepts](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-data)

questions.stem: An engineer wants a framework-agnostic Python prediction interface after loading a registered model. Which MLflow flavor should they prefer?

options.text: `mlflow.pyfunc.load_model`
`mlflow.tensorflow.load_model` for every model
`mlflow.sklearn.load_model` for every model
A raw blob download with no MLflow flavor

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because The pyfunc flavor provides a generic prediction interface independent of the original training library.

**B** is incorrect because The TensorFlow flavor is framework specific.

**C** is incorrect because The sklearn flavor is framework specific.

**D** is incorrect because Skipping MLflow flavors loses the generic packaged interface.

[Deploy MLflow models in Azure ML](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-deploy-mlflow-models?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: What is the key advantage of a managed online endpoint in Azure ML?

options.text: It supports real-time inference with deployment management handled by Azure ML
It is only for offline scoring of very large datasets
It cannot route traffic between deployments
It requires Kubernetes administration by default

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Managed online endpoints are Azure-managed real-time serving endpoints.

**B** is incorrect because Offline scoring is the role of batch endpoints.

**C** is incorrect because Traffic splitting is supported across deployments.

**D** is incorrect because Managed endpoints reduce the need for direct cluster administration.

[Azure ML online endpoints](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-endpoints-online?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: A team wants blue-green rollout for a new model version with 10% traffic before full cutover. Which capability supports that?

options.text: Multiple deployments behind one online endpoint with traffic splitting
A batch endpoint job schedule
A Responsible AI dashboard widget
A datastore secret rotation

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Online endpoints support multiple deployments and configurable traffic percentages for phased rollout.

**B** is incorrect because Batch endpoints are asynchronous and not designed for live traffic split.

**C** is incorrect because The RAI dashboard is for assessment, not request routing.

**D** is incorrect because Secret rotation is unrelated to rollout strategy.

[Azure ML online endpoints](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-endpoints-online?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: When is a batch endpoint usually a better fit than an online endpoint?

options.text: When scoring large datasets asynchronously without a low-latency interactive requirement
When every request must return in milliseconds
When the team needs traffic splitting for web inference
When no input data exists yet

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Batch endpoints are optimized for asynchronous large-scale scoring.

**B** is incorrect because Millisecond interactive requests point to online endpoints.

**C** is incorrect because Traffic splitting is an online endpoint pattern.

**D** is incorrect because Endpoints do not solve the absence of input data.

[Azure ML batch endpoints](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-endpoints-batch?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of an Azure ML registry in MLOps?

options.text: To share and govern reusable ML assets such as models and components across workspaces
To serve public HTTPS traffic directly
To replace Git repositories
To run notebook kernels

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Registries centralize reusable assets for promotion and sharing across workspaces.

**B** is incorrect because Serving is handled by endpoints.

**C** is incorrect because Git still manages source control.

**D** is incorrect because Notebook execution is handled by compute resources.

[Azure ML registries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-machine-learning-registries-mlops?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: A model passes evaluation in dev and should move to broader reuse with version history and tags. Which action aligns best with that goal?

options.text: Register or promote the model in a registry with metadata
Create a new local folder on one engineer laptop
Delete the training run
Convert the model to an MLTable

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Registries support versioning, metadata, and controlled promotion of ML assets.

**B** is incorrect because A local folder is not a governed promotion mechanism.

**C** is incorrect because Deleting the run removes lineage.

**D** is incorrect because MLTable is for data assets, not model promotion.

[Azure ML registries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-machine-learning-registries-mlops?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: What does Azure ML model monitoring help detect?

options.text: Data quality or prediction drift over time
Whether a user likes the UI theme
Whether Git branches were named well
Whether Docker is installed on a laptop

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Model monitoring focuses on data quality, drift, and related production behavior changes.

**B** is incorrect because UI preference is unrelated.

**C** is incorrect because Branch naming is a source-control concern.

**D** is incorrect because Laptop Docker installation is not the purpose of model monitoring.

[Monitor model performance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-monitor-model-performance?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: A production team wants a baseline from training data and scheduled checks with alerts when serving inputs drift materially. Which Azure ML feature set addresses this?

options.text: Model monitoring jobs with configured baselines and alerting integrations
Only notebook comments stored beside the run
A single manual dashboard screenshot
An online endpoint traffic split

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Azure ML monitoring supports scheduled jobs, baselines, and alerting for production drift and quality signals.

**B** is incorrect because Notebook comments are not monitoring automation.

**C** is incorrect because A screenshot is not an operational control.

**D** is incorrect because Traffic split helps rollout but not drift detection itself.

[Monitor model performance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-monitor-model-performance?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: What is the Responsible AI dashboard primarily used for?

options.text: Inspecting fairness, explanations, error patterns, and related model behaviors
Creating Kubernetes clusters
Training foundation models from scratch
Replacing MLflow tracking

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because The Responsible AI dashboard brings together explanations, fairness, error analysis, and similar assessments.

**B** is incorrect because Cluster creation is unrelated.

**C** is incorrect because It does not train foundation models.

**D** is incorrect because It complements rather than replaces experiment tracking.

[Responsible AI dashboard](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-responsible-ai-dashboard?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: A generative workflow built in Azure wants prompt orchestration, testing, and execution of flow graphs. Which tool family is most aligned?

options.text: Prompt flow
Batch endpoints
Model registry tags
Datastore credentials

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Prompt flow is designed for authoring and evaluating prompt-based AI flows.

**B** is incorrect because Batch endpoints are for asynchronous scoring jobs.

**C** is incorrect because Registry tags are metadata only.

**D** is incorrect because Datastore credentials are storage configuration.

[Prompt flow in Azure AI Foundry](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/flow-develop)

questions.stem: Which TWO choices improve reproducibility of Azure ML training jobs? (Select TWO)

options.text: Pin the environment and package dependencies
Keep component and pipeline definitions versioned
Manually edit notebooks in production without source control
Change runtime parameters silently between runs without tracking

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Pinned environments help ensure the same software stack across runs.

**B** is correct because Versioned components and pipelines improve traceability and reuse.

**C** is incorrect because Untracked notebook edits reduce reproducibility.

**D** is incorrect because Untracked parameter changes undermine comparability.

[Azure ML pipelines and components](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-create-component-pipelines-cli)

questions.stem: A bank must host inference inside its own AKS environment because of enterprise network controls. Which endpoint style is the best fit?

options.text: A Kubernetes online endpoint on AKS
A managed online endpoint with no network planning
A batch endpoint only
A registry-only deployment

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Kubernetes online endpoints support AKS-based serving for organizations that need cluster-level control.

**B** is incorrect because Managed endpoints reduce ops but may not satisfy that AKS requirement.

**C** is incorrect because Batch endpoints are asynchronous rather than interactive.

**D** is incorrect because A registry stores assets but does not serve them.

[Azure ML online endpoints](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-endpoints-online?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: Why do Azure ML environments matter in CI/CD?

options.text: They package consistent runtime dependencies for repeatable jobs and deployments
They replace every need for Docker or conda metadata
They are only visual labels in Studio
They can be ignored once a model is registered

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Environments define the runtime stack used by jobs and deployments, which is central to repeatability.

**B** is incorrect because They often rely on Docker and dependency specs rather than replacing them conceptually.

**C** is incorrect because They have operational effect beyond labeling.

**D** is incorrect because Registered models still need a serving environment.

[Azure ML compute targets](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-compute-target)

questions.stem: What is a practical role for GitHub Actions in Azure ML MLOps?

options.text: Automating training, validation, or deployment workflows from repository events
Replacing the Azure ML control plane
Serving real-time predictions directly
Becoming the model registry itself

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because GitHub Actions can orchestrate CI/CD pipelines that trigger Azure ML jobs and deployments.

**B** is incorrect because Azure ML remains the ML platform control plane.

**C** is incorrect because Predictions are served by endpoints.

**D** is incorrect because Actions do not replace a registry.

[Use GitHub Actions with Azure ML](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-github-actions-machine-learning?view=azureml-api-2)

questions.stem: An engineer needs a folder of files as pipeline input with no table semantics. Which data asset abstraction is usually sufficient?

options.text: URI folder
MLTable
Managed online endpoint
Traffic rule

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because A URI folder is the standard abstraction for folder-based file inputs without table behavior.

**B** is incorrect because MLTable is for structured/tabular semantics.

**C** is incorrect because An endpoint is a serving resource, not a dataset abstraction.

**D** is incorrect because Traffic rules apply to deployments, not data assets.

[Azure ML data concepts](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-data)

questions.stem: A pipeline must fan out scoring across partitions and then merge outputs. Which design is most aligned with Azure ML capabilities?

options.text: Use reusable components and parallel-capable pipeline steps
Put every partition in a single monolithic script step by hand
Avoid versioning so each run can change freely
Use only a model registry with no pipeline job

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Azure ML pipelines support reusable components and patterns for parallelizable job design.

**B** is incorrect because A monolithic hand-built step is harder to scale and govern.

**C** is incorrect because Avoiding versioning hurts MLOps discipline.

**D** is incorrect because A registry alone does not execute pipelines.

[Azure ML pipelines and components](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-create-component-pipelines-cli)

**B** is incorrect because Online endpoints serve inference traffic, not notebook authoring.

[Azure ML compute targets](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/concept-compute-target)
